pattern: Sorting turns the strong-pair condition into a sliding window over a prefix, so a binary trie that only ever holds the currently-valid window of candidates can answer 'best xor partner' for each number in turn.
hints: Sort the array; the strong-pair condition |x - y| <= min(x, y) becomes simply 'the larger value is at most twice the smaller' once you fix which one is smaller.
Walk the sorted array left to right as the larger element of each pair, maintaining a sliding window (via a moving left pointer) of the earlier elements that are still within a factor of two.
Keep the current window's elements in a binary trie over their bits, so for each new number you can greedily find the window element that maximizes the xor in time proportional to the bit width.

edge_inputs.description: statement example two, only self-pairs qualify
values at the top of the allowed range
all identical values, best xor is zero
one value more than double every other, isolated

stress.description: random moderate-size arrays spanning the 20-bit value range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 2000))
    nums = [rng.randint(1, (1 << 20) - 1) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]
